verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: एषः — हृदयस्थः आत्मा — ब्रह्म। एतम् — इतः प्रेत्य — अभिसंभवितास्मि — ब्रह्मैव भविष्यामि।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: The Self within the heart is Brahman. After departing from this body, I shall become that Brahman. This is the declaration of the knower of Brahman — the certainty of liberation.

verses.sanskrit: आकाशो वै नाम नामरूपयोर्निर्वहिता | ते यदन्तरा तद्ब्रह्म ||८.१४.१||
verses.transliteration: ākāśo vai nāma nāmarūpayor nirvahitā | te yad antarā tad brahma ||8.14.1||

word_meanings.meaning: space is the revealer of name and form
that which is between them is Brahman

verses.english_translation: Space is indeed the revealer of name and form. That which is between them — that is Brahman.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: आकाशः — नामरूपयोः — निर्वहिता — प्रकाशकः। तयोः अन्तरा — ब्रह्म।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: Space reveals name and form — it is the medium in which all things appear. That which is between name and form — the pure awareness in which they appear — that is Brahman.

verses.sanskrit: य आत्मापहतपाप्मा विजरो विमृत्युर्विशोको विजिघत्सोऽपिपासः सत्यकामः सत्यसंकल्पः ||८.७.१||
verses.transliteration: ya ātmāpahatapāpmā vijaro vimṛtyur viśoko vijighatsoapipāsaḥ satyakāmaḥ satyasaṃkalpaḥ ||8.7.1||

word_meanings.meaning: free from old age and death
whose desire and resolve are truth

verses.english_translation: The Self that is free from sin, free from old age, free from death, free from grief, free from hunger and thirst, whose desire is truth, whose resolve is truth.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: अपहतपाप्मा — पापरहितः। विजरः — जरारहितः। विमृत्युः — मृत्युरहितः। सत्यकामः — सत्यम् एव कामः यस्य।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: This describes the true Self — free from all limitations. The Self is not subject to sin, old age, death, grief, hunger, or thirst. Its desires and resolves are always fulfilled because it is Brahman itself.

verses.sanskrit: तमेतमात्मानं विदित्वा ब्राह्मणाः पुत्रैषणायाश्च वित्तैषणायाश्च लोकैषणायाश्च व्युत्थायाथ भिक्षाचर्यं चरन्ति ||२.२३.१||
verses.transliteration: tam etam ātmānaṃ viditvā brāhmaṇāḥ putraiṣaṇāyāś ca vittaiṣaṇāyāś ca lokaiṣaṇāyāś ca vyutthāyātha bhikṣācaryaṃ caranti ||2.23.1||
verses.english_translation: Having known this Self, the brahmins renounce the desire for sons, the desire for wealth, and the desire for worlds, and live as wandering mendicants.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: पुत्रैषणा — पुत्रस्य इच्छा। वित्तैषणा — धनस्य इच्छा। लोकैषणा — स्वर्गस्य इच्छा। एताः त्यक्त्वा — भिक्षाचर्यम् चरन्ति।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: Having known the Self, the wise renounce the three desires — for sons (continuation), wealth (security), and worlds (heaven). These three desires bind one to samsara. Renouncing them, one lives as a mendicant, seeking only liberation.

verses.sanskrit: यो वै ज्येष्ठं च श्रेष्ठं च वेद ज्येष्ठश्च श्रेष्ठश्च स्वानां भवति | प्राणो वाव ज्येष्ठश्च श्रेष्ठश्च ||५.१.१||
verses.transliteration: yo vai jyeṣṭhaṃ ca śreṣṭhaṃ ca veda jyeṣṭhaś ca śreṣṭhaś ca svānāṃ bhavati | prāṇo vāva jyeṣṭhaś ca śreṣṭhaś ca ||5.1.1||

word_meanings.meaning: prana is the eldest and best

verses.english_translation: One who knows the eldest and best becomes the eldest and best among his own. Prana is indeed the eldest and best.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: ज्येष्ठः — सर्वेषाम् प्रथमः। श्रेष्ठः — सर्वेषाम् उत्तमः। प्राणः — ज्येष्ठः श्रेष्ठः — सर्वेषाम् इन्द्रियाणाम्।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: Prana is the eldest and best among all the senses and faculties. One who knows prana as Brahman becomes the eldest and best — the most honored — among his own people.

verses.sanskrit: उद्गीथस्योपनिषदं वेद | यस्य वेद तस्य वेद ||१.१०.९||
verses.transliteration: udgīthasya upaniṣadaṃ veda | yasya veda tasya veda ||1.10.9||

word_meanings.meaning: the secret teaching of the Udgitha
whatever belongs to that one, he knows

verses.english_translation: One who knows the secret teaching of the Udgitha — whatever belongs to that one, he knows.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: उद्गीथस्य उपनिषत् — ओंकारस्य रहस्यम्। यस्य वेद — तस्य सर्वम् वेद।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: The secret teaching of the Udgitha is the knowledge of Om as Brahman. One who knows this secret knows everything — for Om is the symbol of the all-knowing Brahman.

verses.sanskrit: आदित्यो ब्रह्मेत्यादेशः | तस्योपव्याख्यानम् ||३.१९.१||
verses.transliteration: ādityo brahmety ādeśaḥ | tasyopavyākhyānam ||3.19.1||
verses.english_translation: The Sun is Brahman — this is the teaching. Its explanation follows.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: आदित्यः — सूर्यः — ब्रह्म — इत्यादेशः — उपदेशः।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: The Sun is Brahman — this is the teaching of the Chandogya. The Sun is the most visible manifestation of Brahman in the cosmos. Its light, warmth, and life-giving power are expressions of Brahman's infinite power.

verses.sanskrit: एतदमृतमभयमेतद्ब्रह्म | एतद्ब्रह्मणो नाम सत्यमिति ||८.३.४||
verses.transliteration: etad amṛtam abhayam etad brahma | etad brahmaṇo nāma satyam iti ||8.3.4||

word_meanings.meaning: the name of Brahman is truth

verses.english_translation: This is immortal, this is fearless, this is Brahman. The name of this Brahman is truth.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: एतत् — ब्रह्म — अमृतम् — अभयम्। ब्रह्मणः नाम — सत्यम्।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: Brahman is immortal and fearless. Its name is truth (satya). Truth is the highest name of Brahman because Brahman is the ultimate reality — that which never changes, never perishes.

verses.sanskrit: तत् त्वम् असि श्वेतकेतो ||६.८.७||
verses.transliteration: tat tvam asi śvetaketo ||6.8.7||
verses.english_translation: That thou art, O Shvetaketu.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: तत् — जगत्कारणं ब्रह्म। त्वम् — प्रत्यगात्मा। असि — अभिन्नः।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: 'Tat' is Brahman, the cause of the universe. 'Tvam' is the individual Self. 'Asi' declares their identity. This mahavakya teaches the non-difference of the individual soul and Brahman.

verses.sanskrit: ओमित्येतदक्षरमुद्गीथमुपासीत | ओमिति ह्युद्गायति ||१.१.१||
verses.transliteration: om ity etad akṣaram udgītham upāsīta | om iti hy udgāyati ||1.1.1||
verses.english_translation: One should meditate on the syllable Om as the Udgitha. For one sings the Udgitha beginning with Om.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: उद्गीथः — सामगानस्य प्रधानभागः। ओंकारः तस्य वाचकः।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: The Udgitha is the principal part of Sama chanting. Om is its indicator. One should meditate on Om as the Udgitha, knowing it as Brahman.

verses.sanskrit: सर्वं खल्विदं ब्रह्म | तज्जलानिति शान्त उपासीत ||३.१४.१||
verses.transliteration: sarvaṃ khalv idaṃ brahma | tajjalāniti śānta upāsīta ||3.14.1||

word_meanings.meaning: from it born, into it dissolved, in it breathing

verses.english_translation: All this is indeed Brahman. From it all is born, into it all dissolves, in it all breathes. Meditate on it peacefully.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: सर्वम् — जगत् — ब्रह्मैव। तज्जलान् — तस्मात् जायते — तस्मिन् लीयते — तस्मिन् श्वसिति।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: All this universe is Brahman alone. 'Tajjalan' means: from it (taj) all is born (ja), into it all dissolves (la), in it all breathes (an). Meditate on this Brahman peacefully.

verses.sanskrit: मनोमयः प्राणशरीरो भारूपः सत्यसंकल्प आकाशात्मा सर्वकर्मा सर्वकामः ||३.१४.२||
verses.transliteration: manomayaḥ prāṇaśarīro bhārūpaḥ satyasaṃkalpa ākāśātmā sarvakarmā sarvakāmaḥ ||3.14.2||
verses.english_translation: Made of mind, whose body is prana, whose form is light, whose resolve is truth, whose Self is space, who performs all actions, who fulfills all desires.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: मनोमयः — मनःस्वरूपः। सत्यसंकल्पः — यस्य संकल्पः सत्यः। आकाशात्मा — आकाशस्वरूपः।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: This describes the inner Self (Brahman) as made of mind, with prana as its body, light as its form, truth as its resolve, and space as its Self. This is the meditation on the Self within the heart.

verses.sanskrit: एष म आत्मान्तर्हृदये | एतद्ब्रह्म | एतमितः प्रेत्याभिसंभवितास्मि ||३.१४.४||
verses.transliteration: eṣa ma ātmāntar hṛdaye | etad brahma | etam itaḥ pretyābhisaṃbhavitāsmi ||3.14.4||

word_meanings.meaning: this is my Self within the heart

verses.english_translation: This is my Self within the heart. This is Brahman. Into this I shall enter after departing from here.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: एषः — हृदयस्थः आत्मा — ब्रह्म। एतम् — इतः प्रेत्य — अभिसंभवितास्मि — ब्रह्मैव भविष्यामि।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: The Self within the heart is Brahman. After departing from this body, I shall become that Brahman. This is the declaration of the knower of Brahman — the certainty of liberation.

verses.sanskrit: आकाशो वै नाम नामरूपयोर्निर्वहिता | ते यदन्तरा तद्ब्रह्म ||८.१४.१||
verses.transliteration: ākāśo vai nāma nāmarūpayor nirvahitā | te yad antarā tad brahma ||8.14.1||

word_meanings.meaning: space is the revealer of name and form
that which is between them is Brahman

verses.english_translation: Space is indeed the revealer of name and form. That which is between them — that is Brahman.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: आकाशः — नामरूपयोः — निर्वहिता — प्रकाशकः। तयोः अन्तरा — ब्रह्म।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: Space reveals name and form — it is the medium in which all things appear. That which is between name and form — the pure awareness in which they appear — that is Brahman.

verses.sanskrit: य आत्मापहतपाप्मा विजरो विमृत्युर्विशोको विजिघत्सोऽपिपासः सत्यकामः सत्यसंकल्पः ||८.७.१||
verses.transliteration: ya ātmāpahatapāpmā vijaro vimṛtyur viśoko vijighatsoapipāsaḥ satyakāmaḥ satyasaṃkalpaḥ ||8.7.1||

word_meanings.meaning: free from old age and death
whose desire and resolve are truth

verses.english_translation: The Self that is free from sin, free from old age, free from death, free from grief, free from hunger and thirst, whose desire is truth, whose resolve is truth.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: अपहतपाप्मा — पापरहितः। विजरः — जरारहितः। विमृत्युः — मृत्युरहितः। सत्यकामः — सत्यम् एव कामः यस्य।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: This describes the true Self — free from all limitations. The Self is not subject to sin, old age, death, grief, hunger, or thirst. Its desires and resolves are always fulfilled because it is Brahman itself.

verses.sanskrit: तमेतमात्मानं विदित्वा ब्राह्मणाः पुत्रैषणायाश्च वित्तैषणायाश्च लोकैषणायाश्च व्युत्थायाथ भिक्षाचर्यं चरन्ति ||२.२३.१||
verses.transliteration: tam etam ātmānaṃ viditvā brāhmaṇāḥ putraiṣaṇāyāś ca vittaiṣaṇāyāś ca lokaiṣaṇāyāś ca vyutthāyātha bhikṣācaryaṃ caranti ||2.23.1||
verses.english_translation: Having known this Self, the brahmins renounce the desire for sons, the desire for wealth, and the desire for worlds, and live as wandering mendicants.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: पुत्रैषणा — पुत्रस्य इच्छा। वित्तैषणा — धनस्य इच्छा। लोकैषणा — स्वर्गस्य इच्छा। एताः त्यक्त्वा — भिक्षाचर्यम् चरन्ति।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: Having known the Self, the wise renounce the three desires — for sons (continuation), wealth (security), and worlds (heaven). These three desires bind one to samsara. Renouncing them, one lives as a mendicant, seeking only liberation.

verses.sanskrit: यो वै ज्येष्ठं च श्रेष्ठं च वेद ज्येष्ठश्च श्रेष्ठश्च स्वानां भवति | प्राणो वाव ज्येष्ठश्च श्रेष्ठश्च ||५.१.१||
verses.transliteration: yo vai jyeṣṭhaṃ ca śreṣṭhaṃ ca veda jyeṣṭhaś ca śreṣṭhaś ca svānāṃ bhavati | prāṇo vāva jyeṣṭhaś ca śreṣṭhaś ca ||5.1.1||

word_meanings.meaning: prana is the eldest and best

verses.english_translation: One who knows the eldest and best becomes the eldest and best among his own. Prana is indeed the eldest and best.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: ज्येष्ठः — सर्वेषाम् प्रथमः। श्रेष्ठः — सर्वेषाम् उत्तमः। प्राणः — ज्येष्ठः श्रेष्ठः — सर्वेषाम् इन्द्रियाणाम्।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: Prana is the eldest and best among all the senses and faculties. One who knows prana as Brahman becomes the eldest and best — the most honored — among his own people.

verses.sanskrit: उद्गीथस्योपनिषदं वेद | यस्य वेद तस्य वेद ||१.१०.९||
verses.transliteration: udgīthasya upaniṣadaṃ veda | yasya veda tasya veda ||1.10.9||

word_meanings.meaning: the secret teaching of the Udgitha
whatever belongs to that one, he knows

verses.english_translation: One who knows the secret teaching of the Udgitha — whatever belongs to that one, he knows.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: उद्गीथस्य उपनिषत् — ओंकारस्य रहस्यम्। यस्य वेद — तस्य सर्वम् वेद।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: The secret teaching of the Udgitha is the knowledge of Om as Brahman. One who knows this secret knows everything — for Om is the symbol of the all-knowing Brahman.

verses.sanskrit: आदित्यो ब्रह्मेत्यादेशः | तस्योपव्याख्यानम् ||३.१९.१||
verses.transliteration: ādityo brahmety ādeśaḥ | tasyopavyākhyānam ||3.19.1||

verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: आदित्यः — सूर्यः — ब्रह्म — इत्यादेशः — उपदेशः।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: The Sun is Brahman — this is the teaching of the Chandogya. The Sun is the most visible manifestation of Brahman in the cosmos. Its light, warmth, and life-giving power are expressions of Brahman's infinite power.

verses.sanskrit: एतदमृतमभयमेतद्ब्रह्म | एतद्ब्रह्मणो नाम सत्यमिति ||८.३.४||
verses.transliteration: etad amṛtam abhayam etad brahma | etad brahmaṇo nāma satyam iti ||8.3.4||

word_meanings.meaning: the name of Brahman is truth

verses.english_translation: This is immortal, this is fearless, this is Brahman. The name of this Brahman is truth.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: एतत् — ब्रह्म — अमृतम् — अभयम्। ब्रह्मणः नाम — सत्यम्।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: Brahman is immortal and fearless. Its name is truth (satya). Truth is the highest name of Brahman because Brahman is the ultimate reality — that which never changes, never perishes.

verses.sanskrit: तत् त्वम् असि श्वेतकेतो ||६.८.७||
verses.transliteration: tat tvam asi śvetaketo ||6.8.7||
verses.english_translation: That thou art, O Shvetaketu.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: तत् — जगत्कारणं ब्रह्म। त्वम् — प्रत्यगात्मा। असि — अभिन्नः।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: 'Tat' is Brahman, the cause of the universe. 'Tvam' is the individual Self. 'Asi' declares their identity. This mahavakya teaches the non-difference of the individual soul and Brahman.

verses.sanskrit: ओमित्येतदक्षरमुद्गीथमुपासीत | ओमिति ह्युद्गायति ||१.१.१||
verses.transliteration: om ity etad akṣaram udgītham upāsīta | om iti hy udgāyati ||1.1.1||
verses.english_translation: One should meditate on the syllable Om as the Udgitha. For one sings the Udgitha beginning with Om.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: उद्गीथः — सामगानस्य प्रधानभागः। ओंकारः तस्य वाचकः।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: The Udgitha is the principal part of Sama chanting. Om is its indicator. One should meditate on Om as the Udgitha, knowing it as Brahman.

verses.sanskrit: सर्वं खल्विदं ब्रह्म | तज्जलानिति शान्त उपासीत ||३.१४.१||
verses.transliteration: sarvaṃ khalv idaṃ brahma | tajjalāniti śānta upāsīta ||3.14.1||

word_meanings.meaning: from it born, into it dissolved, in it breathing

verses.english_translation: All this is indeed Brahman. From it all is born, into it all dissolves, in it all breathes. Meditate on it peacefully.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: सर्वम् — जगत् — ब्रह्मैव। तज्जलान् — तस्मात् जायते — तस्मिन् लीयते — तस्मिन् श्वसिति।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: All this universe is Brahman alone. 'Tajjalan' means: from it (taj) all is born (ja), into it all dissolves (la), in it all breathes (an). Meditate on this Brahman peacefully.

verses.sanskrit: मनोमयः प्राणशरीरो भारूपः सत्यसंकल्प आकाशात्मा सर्वकर्मा सर्वकामः ||३.१४.२||
verses.transliteration: manomayaḥ prāṇaśarīro bhārūpaḥ satyasaṃkalpa ākāśātmā sarvakarmā sarvakāmaḥ ||3.14.2||
verses.english_translation: Made of mind, whose body is prana, whose form is light, whose resolve is truth, whose Self is space, who performs all actions, who fulfills all desires.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: मनोमयः — मनःस्वरूपः। सत्यसंकल्पः — यस्य संकल्पः सत्यः। आकाशात्मा — आकाशस्वरूपः।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: This describes the inner Self (Brahman) as made of mind, with prana as its body, light as its form, truth as its resolve, and space as its Self. This is the meditation on the Self within the heart.

verses.sanskrit: एष म आत्मान्तर्हृदये | एतद्ब्रह्म | एतमितः प्रेत्याभिसंभवितास्मि ||३.१४.४||
verses.transliteration: eṣa ma ātmāntar hṛdaye | etad brahma | etam itaḥ pretyābhisaṃbhavitāsmi ||3.14.4||

word_meanings.meaning: this is my Self within the heart

verses.english_translation: This is my Self within the heart. This is Brahman. Into this I shall enter after departing from here.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: एषः — हृदयस्थः आत्मा — ब्रह्म। एतम् — इतः प्रेत्य — अभिसंभवितास्मि — ब्रह्मैव भविष्यामि।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: The Self within the heart is Brahman. After departing from this body, I shall become that Brahman. This is the declaration of the knower of Brahman — the certainty of liberation.

verses.sanskrit: आकाशो वै नाम नामरूपयोर्निर्वहिता | ते यदन्तरा तद्ब्रह्म ||८.१४.१||
verses.transliteration: ākāśo vai nāma nāmarūpayor nirvahitā | te yad antarā tad brahma ||8.14.1||

word_meanings.meaning: space is the revealer of name and form
that which is between them is Brahman

verses.english_translation: Space is indeed the revealer of name and form. That which is between them — that is Brahman.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: आकाशः — नामरूपयोः — निर्वहिता — प्रकाशकः। तयोः अन्तरा — ब्रह्म।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: Space reveals name and form — it is the medium in which all things appear. That which is between name and form — the pure awareness in which they appear — that is Brahman.

verses.sanskrit: य आत्मापहतपाप्मा विजरो विमृत्युर्विशोको विजिघत्सोऽपिपासः सत्यकामः सत्यसंकल्पः ||८.७.१||
verses.transliteration: ya ātmāpahatapāpmā vijaro vimṛtyur viśoko vijighatsoapipāsaḥ satyakāmaḥ satyasaṃkalpaḥ ||8.7.1||

word_meanings.meaning: free from old age and death
whose desire and resolve are truth

verses.english_translation: The Self that is free from sin, free from old age, free from death, free from grief, free from hunger and thirst, whose desire is truth, whose resolve is truth.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: अपहतपाप्मा — पापरहितः। विजरः — जरारहितः। विमृत्युः — मृत्युरहितः। सत्यकामः — सत्यम् एव कामः यस्य।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: This describes the true Self — free from all limitations. The Self is not subject to sin, old age, death, grief, hunger, or thirst. Its desires and resolves are always fulfilled because it is Brahman itself.

verses.sanskrit: तमेतमात्मानं विदित्वा ब्राह्मणाः पुत्रैषणायाश्च वित्तैषणायाश्च लोकैषणायाश्च व्युत्थायाथ भिक्षाचर्यं चरन्ति ||२.२३.१||
verses.transliteration: tam etam ātmānaṃ viditvā brāhmaṇāḥ putraiṣaṇāyāś ca vittaiṣaṇāyāś ca lokaiṣaṇāyāś ca vyutthāyātha bhikṣācaryaṃ caranti ||2.23.1||
verses.english_translation: Having known this Self, the brahmins renounce the desire for sons, the desire for wealth, and the desire for worlds, and live as wandering mendicants.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: पुत्रैषणा — पुत्रस्य इच्छा। वित्तैषणा — धनस्य इच्छा। लोकैषणा — स्वर्गस्य इच्छा। एताः त्यक्त्वा — भिक्षाचर्यम् चरन्ति।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: Having known the Self, the wise renounce the three desires — for sons (continuation), wealth (security), and worlds (heaven). These three desires bind one to samsara. Renouncing them, one lives as a mendicant, seeking only liberation.

verses.sanskrit: यो वै ज्येष्ठं च श्रेष्ठं च वेद ज्येष्ठश्च श्रेष्ठश्च स्वानां भवति | प्राणो वाव ज्येष्ठश्च श्रेष्ठश्च ||५.१.१||
verses.transliteration: yo vai jyeṣṭhaṃ ca śreṣṭhaṃ ca veda jyeṣṭhaś ca śreṣṭhaś ca svānāṃ bhavati | prāṇo vāva jyeṣṭhaś ca śreṣṭhaś ca ||5.1.1||

word_meanings.meaning: prana is the eldest and best

verses.english_translation: One who knows the eldest and best becomes the eldest and best among his own. Prana is indeed the eldest and best.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: ज्येष्ठः — सर्वेषाम् प्रथमः। श्रेष्ठः — सर्वेषाम् उत्तमः। प्राणः — ज्येष्ठः श्रेष्ठः — सर्वेषाम् इन्द्रियाणाम्।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: Prana is the eldest and best among all the senses and faculties. One who knows prana as Brahman becomes the eldest and best — the most honored — among his own people.

verses.sanskrit: उद्गीथस्योपनिषदं वेद | यस्य वेद तस्य वेद ||१.१०.९||
verses.transliteration: udgīthasya upaniṣadaṃ veda | yasya veda tasya veda ||1.10.9||

word_meanings.meaning: the secret teaching of the Udgitha
whatever belongs to that one, he knows

verses.english_translation: One who knows the secret teaching of the Udgitha — whatever belongs to that one, he knows.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: उद्गीथस्य उपनिषत् — ओंकारस्य रहस्यम्। यस्य वेद — तस्य सर्वम् वेद।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: The secret teaching of the Udgitha is the knowledge of Om as Brahman. One who knows this secret knows everything — for Om is the symbol of the all-knowing Brahman.

verses.sanskrit: आदित्यो ब्रह्मेत्यादेशः | तस्योपव्याख्यानम् ||३.१९.१||
verses.transliteration: ādityo brahmety ādeśaḥ | tasyopavyākhyānam ||3.19.1||
verses.english_translation: The Sun is Brahman — this is the teaching. Its explanation follows.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: आदित्यः — सूर्यः — ब्रह्म — इत्यादेशः — उपदेशः।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: The Sun is Brahman — this is the teaching of the Chandogya. The Sun is the most visible manifestation of Brahman in the cosmos. Its light, warmth, and life-giving power are expressions of Brahman's infinite power.

verses.sanskrit: एतदमृतमभयमेतद्ब्रह्म | एतद्ब्रह्मणो नाम सत्यमिति ||८.३.४||
verses.transliteration: etad amṛtam abhayam etad brahma | etad brahmaṇo nāma satyam iti ||8.3.4||

word_meanings.meaning: the name of Brahman is truth

verses.english_translation: This is immortal, this is fearless, this is Brahman. The name of this Brahman is truth.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: एतत् — ब्रह्म — अमृतम् — अभयम्। ब्रह्मणः नाम — सत्यम्।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: Brahman is immortal and fearless. Its name is truth (satya). Truth is the highest name of Brahman because Brahman is the ultimate reality — that which never changes, never perishes.

verses.sanskrit: तत् त्वम् असि श्वेतकेतो ||६.८.७||
verses.transliteration: tat tvam asi śvetaketo ||6.8.7||
verses.english_translation: That thou art, O Shvetaketu.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: तत् — जगत्कारणं ब्रह्म। त्वम् — प्रत्यगात्मा। असि — अभिन्नः।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: 'Tat' is Brahman, the cause of the universe. 'Tvam' is the individual Self. 'Asi' declares their identity. This mahavakya teaches the non-difference of the individual soul and Brahman.

verses.sanskrit: ओमित्येतदक्षरमुद्गीथमुपासीत | ओमिति ह्युद्गायति ||१.१.१||
verses.transliteration: om ity etad akṣaram udgītham upāsīta | om iti hy udgāyati ||1.1.1||
verses.english_translation: One should meditate on the syllable Om as the Udgitha. For one sings the Udgitha beginning with Om.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: उद्गीथः — सामगानस्य प्रधानभागः। ओंकारः तस्य वाचकः।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: The Udgitha is the principal part of Sama chanting. Om is its indicator. One should meditate on Om as the Udgitha, knowing it as Brahman.

verses.sanskrit: सर्वं खल्विदं ब्रह्म | तज्जलानिति शान्त उपासीत ||३.१४.१||
verses.transliteration: sarvaṃ khalv idaṃ brahma | tajjalāniti śānta upāsīta ||3.14.1||

word_meanings.meaning: from it born, into it dissolved, in it breathing

verses.english_translation: All this is indeed Brahman. From it all is born, into it all dissolves, in it all breathes. Meditate on it peacefully.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: सर्वम् — जगत् — ब्रह्मैव। तज्जलान् — तस्मात् जायते — तस्मिन् लीयते — तस्मिन् श्वसिति।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: All this universe is Brahman alone. 'Tajjalan' means: from it (taj) all is born (ja), into it all dissolves (la), in it all breathes (an). Meditate on this Brahman peacefully.

verses.sanskrit: मनोमयः प्राणशरीरो भारूपः सत्यसंकल्प आकाशात्मा सर्वकर्मा सर्वकामः ||३.१४.२||
verses.transliteration: manomayaḥ prāṇaśarīro bhārūpaḥ satyasaṃkalpa ākāśātmā sarvakarmā sarvakāmaḥ ||3.14.2||
verses.english_translation: Made of mind, whose body is prana, whose form is light, whose resolve is truth, whose Self is space, who performs all actions, who fulfills all desires.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: मनोमयः — मनःस्वरूपः। सत्यसंकल्पः — यस्य संकल्पः सत्यः। आकाशात्मा — आकाशस्वरूपः।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: This describes the inner Self (Brahman) as made of mind, with prana as its body, light as its form, truth as its resolve, and space as its Self. This is the meditation on the Self within the heart.

verses.sanskrit: एष म आत्मान्तर्हृदये | एतद्ब्रह्म | एतमितः प्रेत्याभिसंभवितास्मि ||३.१४.४||
verses.transliteration: eṣa ma ātmāntar hṛdaye | etad brahma | etam itaḥ pretyābhisaṃbhavitāsmi ||3.14.4||

word_meanings.meaning: this is my Self within the heart

verses.english_translation: This is my Self within the heart. This is Brahman. Into this I shall enter after departing from here.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: एषः — हृदयस्थः आत्मा — ब्रह्म। एतम् — इतः प्रेत्य — अभिसंभवितास्मि — ब्रह्मैव भविष्यामि।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: The Self within the heart is Brahman. After departing from this body, I shall become that Brahman. This is the declaration of the knower of Brahman — the certainty of liberation.

verses.sanskrit: आकाशो वै नाम नामरूपयोर्निर्वहिता | ते यदन्तरा तद्ब्रह्म ||८.१४.१||
verses.transliteration: ākāśo vai nāma nāmarūpayor nirvahitā | te yad antarā tad brahma ||8.14.1||

word_meanings.meaning: space is the revealer of name and form
that which is between them is Brahman

verses.english_translation: Space is indeed the revealer of name and form. That which is between them — that is Brahman.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: आकाशः — नामरूपयोः — निर्वहिता — प्रकाशकः। तयोः अन्तरा — ब्रह्म।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: Space reveals name and form — it is the medium in which all things appear. That which is between name and form — the pure awareness in which they appear — that is Brahman.

verses.sanskrit: य आत्मापहतपाप्मा विजरो विमृत्युर्विशोको विजिघत्सोऽपिपासः सत्यकामः सत्यसंकल्पः ||८.७.१||
verses.transliteration: ya ātmāpahatapāpmā vijaro vimṛtyur viśoko vijighatsoapipāsaḥ satyakāmaḥ satyasaṃkalpaḥ ||8.7.1||

word_meanings.meaning: free from old age and death
whose desire and resolve are truth

verses.english_translation: The Self that is free from sin, free from old age, free from death, free from grief, free from hunger and thirst, whose desire is truth, whose resolve is truth.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: अपहतपाप्मा — पापरहितः। विजरः — जरारहितः। विमृत्युः — मृत्युरहितः। सत्यकामः — सत्यम् एव कामः यस्य।

verses.sanskrit: तमेतमात्मानं विदित्वा ब्राह्मणाः पुत्रैषणायाश्च वित्तैषणायाश्च लोकैषणायाश्च व्युत्थायाथ भिक्षाचर्यं चरन्ति ||२.२३.१||
verses.transliteration: tam etam ātmānaṃ viditvā brāhmaṇāḥ putraiṣaṇāyāś ca vittaiṣaṇāyāś ca lokaiṣaṇāyāś ca vyutthāyātha bhikṣācaryaṃ caranti ||2.23.1||
verses.english_translation: Having known this Self, the brahmins renounce the desire for sons, the desire for wealth, and the desire for worlds, and live as wandering mendicants.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: पुत्रैषणा — पुत्रस्य इच्छा। वित्तैषणा — धनस्य इच्छा। लोकैषणा — स्वर्गस्य इच्छा। एताः त्यक्त्वा — भिक्षाचर्यम् चरन्ति।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: Having known the Self, the wise renounce the three desires — for sons (continuation), wealth (security), and worlds (heaven). These three desires bind one to samsara. Renouncing them, one lives as a mendicant, seeking only liberation.

verses.sanskrit: यो वै ज्येष्ठं च श्रेष्ठं च वेद ज्येष्ठश्च श्रेष्ठश्च स्वानां भवति | प्राणो वाव ज्येष्ठश्च श्रेष्ठश्च ||५.१.१||
verses.transliteration: yo vai jyeṣṭhaṃ ca śreṣṭhaṃ ca veda jyeṣṭhaś ca śreṣṭhaś ca svānāṃ bhavati | prāṇo vāva jyeṣṭhaś ca śreṣṭhaś ca ||5.1.1||

word_meanings.meaning: prana is the eldest and best

verses.english_translation: One who knows the eldest and best becomes the eldest and best among his own. Prana is indeed the eldest and best.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: ज्येष्ठः — सर्वेषाम् प्रथमः। श्रेष्ठः — सर्वेषाम् उत्तमः। प्राणः — ज्येष्ठः श्रेष्ठः — सर्वेषाम् इन्द्रियाणाम्।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: Prana is the eldest and best among all the senses and faculties. One who knows prana as Brahman becomes the eldest and best — the most honored — among his own people.

verses.sanskrit: उद्गीथस्योपनिषदं वेद | यस्य वेद तस्य वेद ||१.१०.९||
verses.transliteration: udgīthasya upaniṣadaṃ veda | yasya veda tasya veda ||1.10.9||

word_meanings.meaning: the secret teaching of the Udgitha
whatever belongs to that one, he knows

verses.english_translation: One who knows the secret teaching of the Udgitha — whatever belongs to that one, he knows.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: उद्गीथस्य उपनिषत् — ओंकारस्य रहस्यम्। यस्य वेद — तस्य सर्वम् वेद।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: The secret teaching of the Udgitha is the knowledge of Om as Brahman. One who knows this secret knows everything — for Om is the symbol of the all-knowing Brahman.

verses.transliteration: ādityo brahmety ādeśaḥ | tasyopavyākhyānam ||3.19.1||

verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: आदित्यः — सूर्यः — ब्रह्म — इत्यादेशः — उपदेशः।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: The Sun is Brahman — this is the teaching of the Chandogya. The Sun is the most visible manifestation of Brahman in the cosmos. Its light, warmth, and life-giving power are expressions of Brahman's infinite power.

verses.sanskrit: एतदमृतमभयमेतद्ब्रह्म | एतद्ब्रह्मणो नाम सत्यमिति ||८.३.४||
verses.transliteration: etad amṛtam abhayam etad brahma | etad brahmaṇo nāma satyam iti ||8.3.4||

word_meanings.meaning: the name of Brahman is truth

verses.english_translation: This is immortal, this is fearless, this is Brahman. The name of this Brahman is truth.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: एतत् — ब्रह्म — अमृतम् — अभयम्। ब्रह्मणः नाम — सत्यम्।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: Brahman is immortal and fearless. Its name is truth (satya). Truth is the highest name of Brahman because Brahman is the ultimate reality — that which never changes, never perishes.

verses.sanskrit: तत् त्वम् असि श्वेतकेतो ||६.८.७||
verses.transliteration: tat tvam asi śvetaketo ||6.8.7||
verses.english_translation: That thou art, O Shvetaketu.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: तत् — जगत्कारणं ब्रह्म। त्वम् — प्रत्यगात्मा। असि — अभिन्नः।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: 'Tat' is Brahman, the cause of the universe. 'Tvam' is the individual Self. 'Asi' declares their identity. This mahavakya teaches the non-difference of the individual soul and Brahman.

verses.sanskrit: ओमित्येतदक्षरमुद्गीथमुपासीत | ओमिति ह्युद्गायति ||१.१.१||
verses.transliteration: om ity etad akṣaram udgītham upāsīta | om iti hy udgāyati ||1.1.1||
verses.english_translation: One should meditate on the syllable Om as the Udgitha. For one sings the Udgitha beginning with Om.

word_meanings.meaning: from it born, into it dissolved, in it breathing

verses.english_translation: All this is indeed Brahman. From it all is born, into it all dissolves, in it all breathes. Meditate on it peacefully.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: सर्वम् — जगत् — ब्रह्मैव। तज्जलान् — तस्मात् जायते — तस्मिन् लीयते — तस्मिन् श्वसिति।

verses.sanskrit: मनोमयः प्राणशरीरो भारूपः सत्यसंकल्प आकाशात्मा सर्वकर्मा सर्वकामः ||३.१४.२||
verses.transliteration: manomayaḥ prāṇaśarīro bhārūpaḥ satyasaṃkalpa ākāśātmā sarvakarmā sarvakāmaḥ ||3.14.2||
verses.english_translation: Made of mind, whose body is prana, whose form is light, whose resolve is truth, whose Self is space, who performs all actions, who fulfills all desires.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: मनोमयः — मनःस्वरूपः। सत्यसंकल्पः — यस्य संकल्पः सत्यः। आकाशात्मा — आकाशस्वरूपः।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: This describes the inner Self (Brahman) as made of mind, with prana as its body, light as its form, truth as its resolve, and space as its Self. This is the meditation on the Self within the heart.

verses.sanskrit: एष म आत्मान्तर्हृदये | एतद्ब्रह्म | एतमितः प्रेत्याभिसंभवितास्मि ||३.१४.४||
verses.transliteration: eṣa ma ātmāntar hṛdaye | etad brahma | etam itaḥ pretyābhisaṃbhavitāsmi ||3.14.4||

word_meanings.meaning: this is my Self within the heart

verses.english_translation: This is my Self within the heart. This is Brahman. Into this I shall enter after departing from here.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: एषः — हृदयस्थः आत्मा — ब्रह्म। एतम् — इतः प्रेत्य — अभिसंभवितास्मि — ब्रह्मैव भविष्यामि।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: The Self within the heart is Brahman. After departing from this body, I shall become that Brahman. This is the declaration of the knower of Brahman — the certainty of liberation.

verses.sanskrit: आकाशो वै नाम नामरूपयोर्निर्वहिता | ते यदन्तरा तद्ब्रह्म ||८.१४.१||
verses.transliteration: ākāśo vai nāma nāmarūpayor nirvahitā | te yad antarā tad brahma ||8.14.1||

word_meanings.meaning: space is the revealer of name and form
that which is between them is Brahman

verses.english_translation: Space is indeed the revealer of name and form. That which is between them — that is Brahman.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: आकाशः — नामरूपयोः — निर्वहिता — प्रकाशकः। तयोः अन्तरा — ब्रह्म।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: Space reveals name and form — it is the medium in which all things appear. That which is between name and form — the pure awareness in which they appear — that is Brahman.

verses.sanskrit: य आत्मापहतपाप्मा विजरो विमृत्युर्विशोको विजिघत्सोऽपिपासः सत्यकामः सत्यसंकल्पः ||८.७.१||
verses.transliteration: ya ātmāpahatapāpmā vijaro vimṛtyur viśoko vijighatsoapipāsaḥ satyakāmaḥ satyasaṃkalpaḥ ||8.7.1||

word_meanings.meaning: free from old age and death
whose desire and resolve are truth

verses.english_translation: The Self that is free from sin, free from old age, free from death, free from grief, free from hunger and thirst, whose desire is truth, whose resolve is truth.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: अपहतपाप्मा — पापरहितः। विजरः — जरारहितः। विमृत्युः — मृत्युरहितः। सत्यकामः — सत्यम् एव कामः यस्य।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: This describes the true Self — free from all limitations. The Self is not subject to sin, old age, death, grief, hunger, or thirst. Its desires and resolves are always fulfilled because it is Brahman itself.

verses.sanskrit: तमेतमात्मानं विदित्वा ब्राह्मणाः पुत्रैषणायाश्च वित्तैषणायाश्च लोकैषणायाश्च व्युत्थायाथ भिक्षाचर्यं चरन्ति ||२.२३.१||
verses.transliteration: tam etam ātmānaṃ viditvā brāhmaṇāḥ putraiṣaṇāyāś ca vittaiṣaṇāyāś ca lokaiṣaṇāyāś ca vyutthāyātha bhikṣācaryaṃ caranti ||2.23.1||
verses.english_translation: Having known this Self, the brahmins renounce the desire for sons, the desire for wealth, and the desire for worlds, and live as wandering mendicants.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: पुत्रैषणा — पुत्रस्य इच्छा। वित्तैषणा — धनस्य इच्छा। लोकैषणा — स्वर्गस्य इच्छा। एताः त्यक्त्वा — भिक्षाचर्यम् चरन्ति।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: Having known the Self, the wise renounce the three desires — for sons (continuation), wealth (security), and worlds (heaven). These three desires bind one to samsara. Renouncing them, one lives as a mendicant, seeking only liberation.

verses.sanskrit: यो वै ज्येष्ठं च श्रेष्ठं च वेद ज्येष्ठश्च श्रेष्ठश्च स्वानां भवति | प्राणो वाव ज्येष्ठश्च श्रेष्ठश्च ||५.१.१||
verses.transliteration: yo vai jyeṣṭhaṃ ca śreṣṭhaṃ ca veda jyeṣṭhaś ca śreṣṭhaś ca svānāṃ bhavati | prāṇo vāva jyeṣṭhaś ca śreṣṭhaś ca ||5.1.1||

word_meanings.meaning: prana is the eldest and best

verses.english_translation: One who knows the eldest and best becomes the eldest and best among his own. Prana is indeed the eldest and best.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: ज्येष्ठः — सर्वेषाम् प्रथमः। श्रेष्ठः — सर्वेषाम् उत्तमः। प्राणः — ज्येष्ठः श्रेष्ठः — सर्वेषाम् इन्द्रियाणाम्।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: Prana is the eldest and best among all the senses and faculties. One who knows prana as Brahman becomes the eldest and best — the most honored — among his own people.

verses.sanskrit: उद्गीथस्योपनिषदं वेद | यस्य वेद तस्य वेद ||१.१०.९||
verses.transliteration: udgīthasya upaniṣadaṃ veda | yasya veda tasya veda ||1.10.9||

word_meanings.meaning: the secret teaching of the Udgitha
whatever belongs to that one, he knows

verses.english_translation: One who knows the secret teaching of the Udgitha — whatever belongs to that one, he knows.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: उद्गीथस्य उपनिषत् — ओंकारस्य रहस्यम्। यस्य वेद — तस्य सर्वम् वेद।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: The secret teaching of the Udgitha is the knowledge of Om as Brahman. One who knows this secret knows everything — for Om is the symbol of the all-knowing Brahman.

verses.sanskrit: आदित्यो ब्रह्मेत्यादेशः | तस्योपव्याख्यानम् ||३.१९.१||
verses.transliteration: ādityo brahmety ādeśaḥ | tasyopavyākhyānam ||3.19.1||
verses.english_translation: The Sun is Brahman — this is the teaching. Its explanation follows.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: आदित्यः — सूर्यः — ब्रह्म — इत्यादेशः — उपदेशः।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: The Sun is Brahman — this is the teaching of the Chandogya. The Sun is the most visible manifestation of Brahman in the cosmos. Its light, warmth, and life-giving power are expressions of Brahman's infinite power.

verses.sanskrit: एतदमृतमभयमेतद्ब्रह्म | एतद्ब्रह्मणो नाम सत्यमिति ||८.३.४||
verses.transliteration: etad amṛtam abhayam etad brahma | etad brahmaṇo nāma satyam iti ||8.3.4||

word_meanings.meaning: the name of Brahman is truth

verses.english_translation: This is immortal, this is fearless, this is Brahman. The name of this Brahman is truth.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: एतत् — ब्रह्म — अमृतम् — अभयम्। ब्रह्मणः नाम — सत्यम्।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: Brahman is immortal and fearless. Its name is truth (satya). Truth is the highest name of Brahman because Brahman is the ultimate reality — that which never changes, never perishes.

verses.sanskrit: तत् त्वम् असि श्वेतकेतो ||६.८.७||
verses.transliteration: tat tvam asi śvetaketo ||6.8.7||
verses.english_translation: That thou art, O Shvetaketu.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: तत् — जगत्कारणं ब्रह्म। त्वम् — प्रत्यगात्मा। असि — अभिन्नः।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: 'Tat' is Brahman, the cause of the universe. 'Tvam' is the individual Self. 'Asi' declares their identity. This mahavakya teaches the non-difference of the individual soul and Brahman.

verses.sanskrit: ओमित्येतदक्षरमुद्गीथमुपासीत | ओमिति ह्युद्गायति ||१.१.१||
verses.transliteration: om ity etad akṣaram udgītham upāsīta | om iti hy udgāyati ||1.1.1||
verses.english_translation: One should meditate on the syllable Om as the Udgitha. For one sings the Udgitha beginning with Om.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: उद्गीथः — सामगानस्य प्रधानभागः। ओंकारः तस्य वाचकः।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: The Udgitha is the principal part of Sama chanting. Om is its indicator. One should meditate on Om as the Udgitha, knowing it as Brahman.

verses.sanskrit: सर्वं खल्विदं ब्रह्म | तज्जलानिति शान्त उपासीत ||३.१४.१||
verses.transliteration: sarvaṃ khalv idaṃ brahma | tajjalāniti śānta upāsīta ||3.14.1||

word_meanings.meaning: from it born, into it dissolved, in it breathing

verses.english_translation: All this is indeed Brahman. From it all is born, into it all dissolves, in it all breathes. Meditate on it peacefully.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: सर्वम् — जगत् — ब्रह्मैव। तज्जलान् — तस्मात् जायते — तस्मिन् लीयते — तस्मिन् श्वसिति।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: All this universe is Brahman alone. 'Tajjalan' means: from it (taj) all is born (ja), into it all dissolves (la), in it all breathes (an). Meditate on this Brahman peacefully.

verses.sanskrit: मनोमयः प्राणशरीरो भारूपः सत्यसंकल्प आकाशात्मा सर्वकर्मा सर्वकामः ||३.१४.२||
verses.transliteration: manomayaḥ prāṇaśarīro bhārūpaḥ satyasaṃkalpa ākāśātmā sarvakarmā sarvakāmaḥ ||3.14.2||
verses.english_translation: Made of mind, whose body is prana, whose form is light, whose resolve is truth, whose Self is space, who performs all actions, who fulfills all desires.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: मनोमयः — मनःस्वरूपः। सत्यसंकल्पः — यस्य संकल्पः सत्यः। आकाशात्मा — आकाशस्वरूपः।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: This describes the inner Self (Brahman) as made of mind, with prana as its body, light as its form, truth as its resolve, and space as its Self. This is the meditation on the Self within the heart.

verses.sanskrit: एष म आत्मान्तर्हृदये | एतद्ब्रह्म | एतमितः प्रेत्याभिसंभवितास्मि ||३.१४.४||
verses.transliteration: eṣa ma ātmāntar hṛdaye | etad brahma | etam itaḥ pretyābhisaṃbhavitāsmi ||3.14.4||

word_meanings.meaning: this is my Self within the heart

verses.english_translation: This is my Self within the heart. This is Brahman. Into this I shall enter after departing from here.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: एषः — हृदयस्थः आत्मा — ब्रह्म। एतम् — इतः प्रेत्य — अभिसंभवितास्मि — ब्रह्मैव भविष्यामि।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: The Self within the heart is Brahman. After departing from this body, I shall become that Brahman. This is the declaration of the knower of Brahman — the certainty of liberation.

verses.sanskrit: आकाशो वै नाम नामरूपयोर्निर्वहिता | ते यदन्तरा तद्ब्रह्म ||८.१४.१||
verses.transliteration: ākāśo vai nāma nāmarūpayor nirvahitā | te yad antarā tad brahma ||8.14.1||

word_meanings.meaning: space is the revealer of name and form
that which is between them is Brahman

verses.english_translation: Space is indeed the revealer of name and form. That which is between them — that is Brahman.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: आकाशः — नामरूपयोः — निर्वहिता — प्रकाशकः। तयोः अन्तरा — ब्रह्म।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: Space reveals name and form — it is the medium in which all things appear. That which is between name and form — the pure awareness in which they appear — that is Brahman.

verses.sanskrit: य आत्मापहतपाप्मा विजरो विमृत्युर्विशोको विजिघत्सोऽपिपासः सत्यकामः सत्यसंकल्पः ||८.७.१||
verses.transliteration: ya ātmāpahatapāpmā vijaro vimṛtyur viśoko vijighatsoapipāsaḥ satyakāmaḥ satyasaṃkalpaḥ ||8.7.1||

word_meanings.meaning: free from old age and death
whose desire and resolve are truth

verses.english_translation: The Self that is free from sin, free from old age, free from death, free from grief, free from hunger and thirst, whose desire is truth, whose resolve is truth.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: अपहतपाप्मा — पापरहितः। विजरः — जरारहितः। विमृत्युः — मृत्युरहितः। सत्यकामः — सत्यम् एव कामः यस्य।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: This describes the true Self — free from all limitations. The Self is not subject to sin, old age, death, grief, hunger, or thirst. Its desires and resolves are always fulfilled because it is Brahman itself.

verses.sanskrit: तमेतमात्मानं विदित्वा ब्राह्मणाः पुत्रैषणायाश्च वित्तैषणायाश्च लोकैषणायाश्च व्युत्थायाथ भिक्षाचर्यं चरन्ति ||२.२३.१||
verses.transliteration: tam etam ātmānaṃ viditvā brāhmaṇāḥ putraiṣaṇāyāś ca vittaiṣaṇāyāś ca lokaiṣaṇāyāś ca vyutthāyātha bhikṣācaryaṃ caranti ||2.23.1||
verses.english_translation: Having known this Self, the brahmins renounce the desire for sons, the desire for wealth, and the desire for worlds, and live as wandering mendicants.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: पुत्रैषणा — पुत्रस्य इच्छा। वित्तैषणा — धनस्य इच्छा। लोकैषणा — स्वर्गस्य इच्छा। एताः त्यक्त्वा — भिक्षाचर्यम् चरन्ति।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: Having known the Self, the wise renounce the three desires — for sons (continuation), wealth (security), and worlds (heaven). These three desires bind one to samsara. Renouncing them, one lives as a mendicant, seeking only liberation.

verses.sanskrit: यो वै ज्येष्ठं च श्रेष्ठं च वेद ज्येष्ठश्च श्रेष्ठश्च स्वानां भवति | प्राणो वाव ज्येष्ठश्च श्रेष्ठश्च ||५.१.१||
verses.transliteration: yo vai jyeṣṭhaṃ ca śreṣṭhaṃ ca veda jyeṣṭhaś ca śreṣṭhaś ca svānāṃ bhavati | prāṇo vāva jyeṣṭhaś ca śreṣṭhaś ca ||5.1.1||

word_meanings.meaning: prana is the eldest and best

verses.english_translation: One who knows the eldest and best becomes the eldest and best among his own. Prana is indeed the eldest and best.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: ज्येष्ठः — सर्वेषाम् प्रथमः। श्रेष्ठः — सर्वेषाम् उत्तमः। प्राणः — ज्येष्ठः श्रेष्ठः — सर्वेषाम् इन्द्रियाणाम्।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: Prana is the eldest and best among all the senses and faculties. One who knows prana as Brahman becomes the eldest and best — the most honored — among his own people.

verses.sanskrit: उद्गीथस्योपनिषदं वेद | यस्य वेद तस्य वेद ||१.१०.९||
verses.transliteration: udgīthasya upaniṣadaṃ veda | yasya veda tasya veda ||1.10.9||

word_meanings.meaning: the secret teaching of the Udgitha
whatever belongs to that one, he knows

verses.english_translation: One who knows the secret teaching of the Udgitha — whatever belongs to that one, he knows.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: उद्गीथस्य उपनिषत् — ओंकारस्य रहस्यम्। यस्य वेद — तस्य सर्वम् वेद।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: The secret teaching of the Udgitha is the knowledge of Om as Brahman. One who knows this secret knows everything — for Om is the symbol of the all-knowing Brahman.

verses.sanskrit: आदित्यो ब्रह्मेत्यादेशः | तस्योपव्याख्यानम् ||३.१९.१||
verses.transliteration: ādityo brahmety ādeśaḥ | tasyopavyākhyānam ||3.19.1||
verses.english_translation: The Sun is Brahman — this is the teaching. Its explanation follows.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: आदित्यः — सूर्यः — ब्रह्म — इत्यादेशः — उपदेशः।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: The Sun is Brahman — this is the teaching of the Chandogya. The Sun is the most visible manifestation of Brahman in the cosmos. Its light, warmth, and life-giving power are expressions of Brahman's infinite power.

verses.sanskrit: एतदमृतमभयमेतद्ब्रह्म | एतद्ब्रह्मणो नाम सत्यमिति ||८.३.४||
verses.transliteration: etad amṛtam abhayam etad brahma | etad brahmaṇo nāma satyam iti ||8.3.4||

word_meanings.meaning: the name of Brahman is truth

verses.english_translation: This is immortal, this is fearless, this is Brahman. The name of this Brahman is truth.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: एतत् — ब्रह्म — अमृतम् — अभयम्। ब्रह्मणः नाम — सत्यम्।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: Brahman is immortal and fearless. Its name is truth (satya). Truth is the highest name of Brahman because Brahman is the ultimate reality — that which never changes, never perishes.

verses.sanskrit: तत् त्वम् असि श्वेतकेतो ||६.८.७||
verses.transliteration: tat tvam asi śvetaketo ||6.8.7||
verses.english_translation: That thou art, O Shvetaketu.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: तत् — जगत्कारणं ब्रह्म। त्वम् — प्रत्यगात्मा। असि — अभिन्नः।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: 'Tat' is Brahman, the cause of the universe. 'Tvam' is the individual Self. 'Asi' declares their identity. This mahavakya teaches the non-difference of the individual soul and Brahman.

verses.sanskrit: ओमित्येतदक्षरमुद्गीथमुपासीत | ओमिति ह्युद्गायति ||१.१.१||
verses.transliteration: om ity etad akṣaram udgītham upāsīta | om iti hy udgāyati ||1.1.1||
verses.english_translation: One should meditate on the syllable Om as the Udgitha. For one sings the Udgitha beginning with Om.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: उद्गीथः — सामगानस्य प्रधानभागः। ओंकारः तस्य वाचकः।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: The Udgitha is the principal part of Sama chanting. Om is its indicator. One should meditate on Om as the Udgitha, knowing it as Brahman.

verses.sanskrit: सर्वं खल्विदं ब्रह्म | तज्जलानिति शान्त उपासीत ||३.१४.१||
verses.transliteration: sarvaṃ khalv idaṃ brahma | tajjalāniti śānta upāsīta ||3.14.1||

word_meanings.meaning: from it born, into it dissolved, in it breathing

verses.english_translation: All this is indeed Brahman. From it all is born, into it all dissolves, in it all breathes. Meditate on it peacefully.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: सर्वम् — जगत् — ब्रह्मैव। तज्जलान् — तस्मात् जायते — तस्मिन् लीयते — तस्मिन् श्वसिति।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: All this universe is Brahman alone. 'Tajjalan' means: from it (taj) all is born (ja), into it all dissolves (la), in it all breathes (an). Meditate on this Brahman peacefully.

verses.sanskrit: मनोमयः प्राणशरीरो भारूपः सत्यसंकल्प आकाशात्मा सर्वकर्मा सर्वकामः ||३.१४.२||
verses.transliteration: manomayaḥ prāṇaśarīro bhārūpaḥ satyasaṃkalpa ākāśātmā sarvakarmā sarvakāmaḥ ||3.14.2||
verses.english_translation: Made of mind, whose body is prana, whose form is light, whose resolve is truth, whose Self is space, who performs all actions, who fulfills all desires.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: मनोमयः — मनःस्वरूपः। सत्यसंकल्पः — यस्य संकल्पः सत्यः। आकाशात्मा — आकाशस्वरूपः।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: This describes the inner Self (Brahman) as made of mind, with prana as its body, light as its form, truth as its resolve, and space as its Self. This is the meditation on the Self within the heart.

verses.sanskrit: एष म आत्मान्तर्हृदये | एतद्ब्रह्म | एतमितः प्रेत्याभिसंभवितास्मि ||३.१४.४||
verses.transliteration: eṣa ma ātmāntar hṛdaye | etad brahma | etam itaḥ pretyābhisaṃbhavitāsmi ||3.14.4||

word_meanings.meaning: this is my Self within the heart

verses.english_translation: This is my Self within the heart. This is Brahman. Into this I shall enter after departing from here.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: एषः — हृदयस्थः आत्मा — ब्रह्म। एतम् — इतः प्रेत्य — अभिसंभवितास्मि — ब्रह्मैव भविष्यामि।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: The Self within the heart is Brahman. After departing from this body, I shall become that Brahman. This is the declaration of the knower of Brahman — the certainty of liberation.

verses.sanskrit: आकाशो वै नाम नामरूपयोर्निर्वहिता | ते यदन्तरा तद्ब्रह्म ||८.१४.१||
verses.transliteration: ākāśo vai nāma nāmarūpayor nirvahitā | te yad antarā tad brahma ||8.14.1||

word_meanings.meaning: space is the revealer of name and form
that which is between them is Brahman

verses.english_translation: Space is indeed the revealer of name and form. That which is between them — that is Brahman.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: आकाशः — नामरूपयोः — निर्वहिता — प्रकाशकः। तयोः अन्तरा — ब्रह्म।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: Space reveals name and form — it is the medium in which all things appear. That which is between name and form — the pure awareness in which they appear — that is Brahman.

verses.sanskrit: य आत्मापहतपाप्मा विजरो विमृत्युर्विशोको विजिघत्सोऽपिपासः सत्यकामः सत्यसंकल्पः ||८.७.१||
verses.transliteration: ya ātmāpahatapāpmā vijaro vimṛtyur viśoko vijighatsoapipāsaḥ satyakāmaḥ satyasaṃkalpaḥ ||8.7.1||

word_meanings.meaning: free from old age and death
whose desire and resolve are truth

verses.english_translation: The Self that is free from sin, free from old age, free from death, free from grief, free from hunger and thirst, whose desire is truth, whose resolve is truth.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: अपहतपाप्मा — पापरहितः। विजरः — जरारहितः। विमृत्युः — मृत्युरहितः। सत्यकामः — सत्यम् एव कामः यस्य।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: This describes the true Self — free from all limitations. The Self is not subject to sin, old age, death, grief, hunger, or thirst. Its desires and resolves are always fulfilled because it is Brahman itself.

verses.sanskrit: तमेतमात्मानं विदित्वा ब्राह्मणाः पुत्रैषणायाश्च वित्तैषणायाश्च लोकैषणायाश्च व्युत्थायाथ भिक्षाचर्यं चरन्ति ||२.२३.१||
verses.transliteration: tam etam ātmānaṃ viditvā brāhmaṇāḥ putraiṣaṇāyāś ca vittaiṣaṇāyāś ca lokaiṣaṇāyāś ca vyutthāyātha bhikṣācaryaṃ caranti ||2.23.1||
verses.english_translation: Having known this Self, the brahmins renounce the desire for sons, the desire for wealth, and the desire for worlds, and live as wandering mendicants.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: पुत्रैषणा — पुत्रस्य इच्छा। वित्तैषणा — धनस्य इच्छा। लोकैषणा — स्वर्गस्य इच्छा। एताः त्यक्त्वा — भिक्षाचर्यम् चरन्ति।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: Having known the Self, the wise renounce the three desires — for sons (continuation), wealth (security), and worlds (heaven). These three desires bind one to samsara. Renouncing them, one lives as a mendicant, seeking only liberation.

verses.sanskrit: यो वै ज्येष्ठं च श्रेष्ठं च वेद ज्येष्ठश्च श्रेष्ठश्च स्वानां भवति | प्राणो वाव ज्येष्ठश्च श्रेष्ठश्च ||५.१.१||
verses.transliteration: yo vai jyeṣṭhaṃ ca śreṣṭhaṃ ca veda jyeṣṭhaś ca śreṣṭhaś ca svānāṃ bhavati | prāṇo vāva jyeṣṭhaś ca śreṣṭhaś ca ||5.1.1||

word_meanings.meaning: prana is the eldest and best

verses.english_translation: One who knows the eldest and best becomes the eldest and best among his own. Prana is indeed the eldest and best.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: ज्येष्ठः — सर्वेषाम् प्रथमः। श्रेष्ठः — सर्वेषाम् उत्तमः। प्राणः — ज्येष्ठः श्रेष्ठः — सर्वेषाम् इन्द्रियाणाम्।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: Prana is the eldest and best among all the senses and faculties. One who knows prana as Brahman becomes the eldest and best — the most honored — among his own people.

verses.sanskrit: उद्गीथस्योपनिषदं वेद | यस्य वेद तस्य वेद ||१.१०.९||
verses.transliteration: udgīthasya upaniṣadaṃ veda | yasya veda tasya veda ||1.10.9||

word_meanings.meaning: the secret teaching of the Udgitha
whatever belongs to that one, he knows

verses.english_translation: One who knows the secret teaching of the Udgitha — whatever belongs to that one, he knows.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: उद्गीथस्य उपनिषत् — ओंकारस्य रहस्यम्। यस्य वेद — तस्य सर्वम् वेद।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: The secret teaching of the Udgitha is the knowledge of Om as Brahman. One who knows this secret knows everything — for Om is the symbol of the all-knowing Brahman.

verses.sanskrit: आदित्यो ब्रह्मेत्यादेशः | तस्योपव्याख्यानम् ||३.१९.१||
verses.transliteration: ādityo brahmety ādeśaḥ | tasyopavyākhyānam ||3.19.1||
verses.english_translation: The Sun is Brahman — this is the teaching. Its explanation follows.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: आदित्यः — सूर्यः — ब्रह्म — इत्यादेशः — उपदेशः।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: The Sun is Brahman — this is the teaching of the Chandogya. The Sun is the most visible manifestation of Brahman in the cosmos. Its light, warmth, and life-giving power are expressions of Brahman's infinite power.

verses.sanskrit: एतदमृतमभयमेतद्ब्रह्म | एतद्ब्रह्मणो नाम सत्यमिति ||८.३.४||
verses.transliteration: etad amṛtam abhayam etad brahma | etad brahmaṇo nāma satyam iti ||8.3.4||

word_meanings.meaning: the name of Brahman is truth

verses.english_translation: This is immortal, this is fearless, this is Brahman. The name of this Brahman is truth.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: एतत् — ब्रह्म — अमृतम् — अभयम्। ब्रह्मणः नाम — सत्यम्।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: Brahman is immortal and fearless. Its name is truth (satya). Truth is the highest name of Brahman because Brahman is the ultimate reality — that which never changes, never perishes.

verses.sanskrit: तत् त्वम् असि श्वेतकेतो ||६.८.७||
verses.transliteration: tat tvam asi śvetaketo ||6.8.7||
verses.english_translation: That thou art, O Shvetaketu.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: तत् — जगत्कारणं ब्रह्म। त्वम् — प्रत्यगात्मा। असि — अभिन्नः।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: 'Tat' is Brahman, the cause of the universe. 'Tvam' is the individual Self. 'Asi' declares their identity. This mahavakya teaches the non-difference of the individual soul and Brahman.

verses.sanskrit: ओमित्येतदक्षरमुद्गीथमुपासीत | ओमिति ह्युद्गायति ||१.१.१||
verses.transliteration: om ity etad akṣaram udgītham upāsīta | om iti hy udgāyati ||1.1.1||
verses.english_translation: One should meditate on the syllable Om as the Udgitha. For one sings the Udgitha beginning with Om.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: उद्गीथः — सामगानस्य प्रधानभागः। ओंकारः तस्य वाचकः।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: The Udgitha is the principal part of Sama chanting. Om is its indicator. One should meditate on Om as the Udgitha, knowing it as Brahman.

verses.sanskrit: सर्वं खल्विदं ब्रह्म | तज्जलानिति शान्त उपासीत ||३.१४.१||
verses.transliteration: sarvaṃ khalv idaṃ brahma | tajjalāniti śānta upāsīta ||3.14.1||

word_meanings.meaning: from it born, into it dissolved, in it breathing

verses.english_translation: All this is indeed Brahman. From it all is born, into it all dissolves, in it all breathes. Meditate on it peacefully.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: सर्वम् — जगत् — ब्रह्मैव। तज्जलान् — तस्मात् जायते — तस्मिन् लीयते — तस्मिन् श्वसिति।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: All this universe is Brahman alone. 'Tajjalan' means: from it (taj) all is born (ja), into it all dissolves (la), in it all breathes (an). Meditate on this Brahman peacefully.

verses.sanskrit: मनोमयः प्राणशरीरो भारूपः सत्यसंकल्प आकाशात्मा सर्वकर्मा सर्वकामः ||३.१४.२||
verses.transliteration: manomayaḥ prāṇaśarīro bhārūpaḥ satyasaṃkalpa ākāśātmā sarvakarmā sarvakāmaḥ ||3.14.2||
verses.english_translation: Made of mind, whose body is prana, whose form is light, whose resolve is truth, whose Self is space, who performs all actions, who fulfills all desires.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: मनोमयः — मनःस्वरूपः। सत्यसंकल्पः — यस्य संकल्पः सत्यः। आकाशात्मा — आकाशस्वरूपः।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: This describes the inner Self (Brahman) as made of mind, with prana as its body, light as its form, truth as its resolve, and space as its Self. This is the meditation on the Self within the heart.

verses.sanskrit: एष म आत्मान्तर्हृदये | एतद्ब्रह्म | एतमितः प्रेत्याभिसंभवितास्मि ||३.१४.४||
verses.transliteration: eṣa ma ātmāntar hṛdaye | etad brahma | etam itaḥ pretyābhisaṃbhavitāsmi ||3.14.4||

word_meanings.meaning: this is my Self within the heart

verses.english_translation: This is my Self within the heart. This is Brahman. Into this I shall enter after departing from here.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: एषः — हृदयस्थः आत्मा — ब्रह्म। एतम् — इतः प्रेत्य — अभिसंभवितास्मि — ब्रह्मैव भविष्यामि।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: The Self within the heart is Brahman. After departing from this body, I shall become that Brahman. This is the declaration of the knower of Brahman — the certainty of liberation.

verses.sanskrit: आकाशो वै नाम नामरूपयोर्निर्वहिता | ते यदन्तरा तद्ब्रह्म ||८.१४.१||
verses.transliteration: ākāśo vai nāma nāmarūpayor nirvahitā | te yad antarā tad brahma ||8.14.1||

that which is between them is Brahman

verses.english_translation: Space is indeed the revealer of name and form. That which is between them — that is Brahman.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: आकाशः — नामरूपयोः — निर्वहिता — प्रकाशकः। तयोः अन्तरा — ब्रह्म।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: Space reveals name and form — it is the medium in which all things appear. That which is between name and form — the pure awareness in which they appear — that is Brahman.

verses.sanskrit: य आत्मापहतपाप्मा विजरो विमृत्युर्विशोको विजिघत्सोऽपिपासः सत्यकामः सत्यसंकल्पः ||८.७.१||
verses.transliteration: ya ātmāpahatapāpmā vijaro vimṛtyur viśoko vijighatsoapipāsaḥ satyakāmaḥ satyasaṃkalpaḥ ||8.7.1||

word_meanings.meaning: free from old age and death
whose desire and resolve are truth

verses.english_translation: The Self that is free from sin, free from old age, free from death, free from grief, free from hunger and thirst, whose desire is truth, whose resolve is truth.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: अपहतपाप्मा — पापरहितः। विजरः — जरारहितः। विमृत्युः — मृत्युरहितः। सत्यकामः — सत्यम् एव कामः यस्य।

verses.transliteration: tam etam ātmānaṃ viditvā brāhmaṇāḥ putraiṣaṇāyāś ca vittaiṣaṇāyāś ca lokaiṣaṇāyāś ca vyutthāyātha bhikṣācaryaṃ caranti ||2.23.1||

verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: पुत्रैषणा — पुत्रस्य इच्छा। वित्तैषणा — धनस्य इच्छा। लोकैषणा — स्वर्गस्य इच्छा। एताः त्यक्त्वा — भिक्षाचर्यम् चरन्ति।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: Having known the Self, the wise renounce the three desires — for sons (continuation), wealth (security), and worlds (heaven). These three desires bind one to samsara. Renouncing them, one lives as a mendicant, seeking only liberation.

verses.sanskrit: यो वै ज्येष्ठं च श्रेष्ठं च वेद ज्येष्ठश्च श्रेष्ठश्च स्वानां भवति | प्राणो वाव ज्येष्ठश्च श्रेष्ठश्च ||५.१.१||
verses.transliteration: yo vai jyeṣṭhaṃ ca śreṣṭhaṃ ca veda jyeṣṭhaś ca śreṣṭhaś ca svānāṃ bhavati | prāṇo vāva jyeṣṭhaś ca śreṣṭhaś ca ||5.1.1||

word_meanings.meaning: prana is the eldest and best

verses.english_translation: One who knows the eldest and best becomes the eldest and best among his own. Prana is indeed the eldest and best.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: ज्येष्ठः — सर्वेषाम् प्रथमः। श्रेष्ठः — सर्वेषाम् उत्तमः। प्राणः — ज्येष्ठः श्रेष्ठः — सर्वेषाम् इन्द्रियाणाम्।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: Prana is the eldest and best among all the senses and faculties. One who knows prana as Brahman becomes the eldest and best — the most honored — among his own people.

verses.sanskrit: उद्गीथस्योपनिषदं वेद | यस्य वेद तस्य वेद ||१.१०.९||
verses.transliteration: udgīthasya upaniṣadaṃ veda | yasya veda tasya veda ||1.10.9||

word_meanings.meaning: the secret teaching of the Udgitha
whatever belongs to that one, he knows

verses.english_translation: One who knows the secret teaching of the Udgitha — whatever belongs to that one, he knows.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: उद्गीथस्य उपनिषत् — ओंकारस्य रहस्यम्। यस्य वेद — तस्य सर्वम् वेद।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: The secret teaching of the Udgitha is the knowledge of Om as Brahman. One who knows this secret knows everything — for Om is the symbol of the all-knowing Brahman.

verses.sanskrit: आदित्यो ब्रह्मेत्यादेशः | तस्योपव्याख्यानम् ||३.१९.१||
verses.transliteration: ādityo brahmety ādeśaḥ | tasyopavyākhyānam ||3.19.1||
verses.english_translation: The Sun is Brahman — this is the teaching. Its explanation follows.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: आदित्यः — सूर्यः — ब्रह्म — इत्यादेशः — उपदेशः।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: The Sun is Brahman — this is the teaching of the Chandogya. The Sun is the most visible manifestation of Brahman in the cosmos. Its light, warmth, and life-giving power are expressions of Brahman's infinite power.

verses.sanskrit: एतदमृतमभयमेतद्ब्रह्म | एतद्ब्रह्मणो नाम सत्यमिति ||८.३.४||
verses.transliteration: etad amṛtam abhayam etad brahma | etad brahmaṇo nāma satyam iti ||8.3.4||

word_meanings.meaning: the name of Brahman is truth

verses.english_translation: This is immortal, this is fearless, this is Brahman. The name of this Brahman is truth.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: एतत् — ब्रह्म — अमृतम् — अभयम्। ब्रह्मणः नाम — सत्यम्।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: Brahman is immortal and fearless. Its name is truth (satya). Truth is the highest name of Brahman because Brahman is the ultimate reality — that which never changes, never perishes.

verses.sanskrit: तत् त्वम् असि श्वेतकेतो ||६.८.७||
verses.transliteration: tat tvam asi śvetaketo ||6.8.7||
verses.english_translation: That thou art, O Shvetaketu.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: तत् — जगत्कारणं ब्रह्म। त्वम् — प्रत्यगात्मा। असि — अभिन्नः।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: 'Tat' is Brahman, the cause of the universe. 'Tvam' is the individual Self. 'Asi' declares their identity. This mahavakya teaches the non-difference of the individual soul and Brahman.

verses.sanskrit: ओमित्येतदक्षरमुद्गीथमुपासीत | ओमिति ह्युद्गायति ||१.१.१||
verses.transliteration: om ity etad akṣaram udgītham upāsīta | om iti hy udgāyati ||1.1.1||
verses.english_translation: One should meditate on the syllable Om as the Udgitha. For one sings the Udgitha beginning with Om.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: उद्गीथः — सामगानस्य प्रधानभागः। ओंकारः तस्य वाचकः।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: The Udgitha is the principal part of Sama chanting. Om is its indicator. One should meditate on Om as the Udgitha, knowing it as Brahman.

verses.sanskrit: सर्वं खल्विदं ब्रह्म | तज्जलानिति शान्त उपासीत ||३.१४.१||
verses.transliteration: sarvaṃ khalv idaṃ brahma | tajjalāniti śānta upāsīta ||3.14.1||

word_meanings.meaning: from it born, into it dissolved, in it breathing

verses.english_translation: All this is indeed Brahman. From it all is born, into it all dissolves, in it all breathes. Meditate on it peacefully.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: सर्वम् — जगत् — ब्रह्मैव। तज्जलान् — तस्मात् जायते — तस्मिन् लीयते — तस्मिन् श्वसिति।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: All this universe is Brahman alone. 'Tajjalan' means: from it (taj) all is born (ja), into it all dissolves (la), in it all breathes (an). Meditate on this Brahman peacefully.

verses.sanskrit: मनोमयः प्राणशरीरो भारूपः सत्यसंकल्प आकाशात्मा सर्वकर्मा सर्वकामः ||३.१४.२||
verses.transliteration: manomayaḥ prāṇaśarīro bhārūpaḥ satyasaṃkalpa ākāśātmā sarvakarmā sarvakāmaḥ ||3.14.2||
verses.english_translation: Made of mind, whose body is prana, whose form is light, whose resolve is truth, whose Self is space, who performs all actions, who fulfills all desires.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: मनोमयः — मनःस्वरूपः। सत्यसंकल्पः — यस्य संकल्पः सत्यः। आकाशात्मा — आकाशस्वरूपः।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: This describes the inner Self (Brahman) as made of mind, with prana as its body, light as its form, truth as its resolve, and space as its Self. This is the meditation on the Self within the heart.

verses.sanskrit: एष म आत्मान्तर्हृदये | एतद्ब्रह्म | एतमितः प्रेत्याभिसंभवितास्मि ||३.१४.४||
verses.transliteration: eṣa ma ātmāntar hṛdaye | etad brahma | etam itaḥ pretyābhisaṃbhavitāsmi ||3.14.4||

word_meanings.meaning: this is my Self within the heart

verses.english_translation: This is my Self within the heart. This is Brahman. Into this I shall enter after departing from here.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: एषः — हृदयस्थः आत्मा — ब्रह्म। एतम् — इतः प्रेत्य — अभिसंभवितास्मि — ब्रह्मैव भविष्यामि।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: The Self within the heart is Brahman. After departing from this body, I shall become that Brahman. This is the declaration of the knower of Brahman — the certainty of liberation.

verses.sanskrit: आकाशो वै नाम नामरूपयोर्निर्वहिता | ते यदन्तरा तद्ब्रह्म ||८.१४.१||
verses.transliteration: ākāśo vai nāma nāmarūpayor nirvahitā | te yad antarā tad brahma ||8.14.1||

word_meanings.meaning: space is the revealer of name and form
that which is between them is Brahman

verses.english_translation: Space is indeed the revealer of name and form. That which is between them — that is Brahman.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: आकाशः — नामरूपयोः — निर्वहिता — प्रकाशकः। तयोः अन्तरा — ब्रह्म।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: Space reveals name and form — it is the medium in which all things appear. That which is between name and form — the pure awareness in which they appear — that is Brahman.

verses.sanskrit: य आत्मापहतपाप्मा विजरो विमृत्युर्विशोको विजिघत्सोऽपिपासः सत्यकामः सत्यसंकल्पः ||८.७.१||
verses.transliteration: ya ātmāpahatapāpmā vijaro vimṛtyur viśoko vijighatsoapipāsaḥ satyakāmaḥ satyasaṃkalpaḥ ||8.7.1||

word_meanings.meaning: free from old age and death
whose desire and resolve are truth

verses.english_translation: The Self that is free from sin, free from old age, free from death, free from grief, free from hunger and thirst, whose desire is truth, whose resolve is truth.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: अपहतपाप्मा — पापरहितः। विजरः — जरारहितः। विमृत्युः — मृत्युरहितः। सत्यकामः — सत्यम् एव कामः यस्य।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: This describes the true Self — free from all limitations. The Self is not subject to sin, old age, death, grief, hunger, or thirst. Its desires and resolves are always fulfilled because it is Brahman itself.

verses.sanskrit: तमेतमात्मानं विदित्वा ब्राह्मणाः पुत्रैषणायाश्च वित्तैषणायाश्च लोकैषणायाश्च व्युत्थायाथ भिक्षाचर्यं चरन्ति ||२.२३.१||
verses.transliteration: tam etam ātmānaṃ viditvā brāhmaṇāḥ putraiṣaṇāyāś ca vittaiṣaṇāyāś ca lokaiṣaṇāyāś ca vyutthāyātha bhikṣācaryaṃ caranti ||2.23.1||
verses.english_translation: Having known this Self, the brahmins renounce the desire for sons, the desire for wealth, and the desire for worlds, and live as wandering mendicants.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: पुत्रैषणा — पुत्रस्य इच्छा। वित्तैषणा — धनस्य इच्छा। लोकैषणा — स्वर्गस्य इच्छा। एताः त्यक्त्वा — भिक्षाचर्यम् चरन्ति।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: Having known the Self, the wise renounce the three desires — for sons (continuation), wealth (security), and worlds (heaven). These three desires bind one to samsara. Renouncing them, one lives as a mendicant, seeking only liberation.

verses.sanskrit: यो वै ज्येष्ठं च श्रेष्ठं च वेद ज्येष्ठश्च श्रेष्ठश्च स्वानां भवति | प्राणो वाव ज्येष्ठश्च श्रेष्ठश्च ||५.१.१||
verses.transliteration: yo vai jyeṣṭhaṃ ca śreṣṭhaṃ ca veda jyeṣṭhaś ca śreṣṭhaś ca svānāṃ bhavati | prāṇo vāva jyeṣṭhaś ca śreṣṭhaś ca ||5.1.1||

word_meanings.meaning: prana is the eldest and best

verses.english_translation: One who knows the eldest and best becomes the eldest and best among his own. Prana is indeed the eldest and best.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: ज्येष्ठः — सर्वेषाम् प्रथमः। श्रेष्ठः — सर्वेषाम् उत्तमः। प्राणः — ज्येष्ठः श्रेष्ठः — सर्वेषाम् इन्द्रियाणाम्।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: Prana is the eldest and best among all the senses and faculties. One who knows prana as Brahman becomes the eldest and best — the most honored — among his own people.

verses.sanskrit: उद्गीथस्योपनिषदं वेद | यस्य वेद तस्य वेद ||१.१०.९||
verses.transliteration: udgīthasya upaniṣadaṃ veda | yasya veda tasya veda ||1.10.9||

word_meanings.meaning: the secret teaching of the Udgitha
whatever belongs to that one, he knows

verses.english_translation: One who knows the secret teaching of the Udgitha — whatever belongs to that one, he knows.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: उद्गीथस्य उपनिषत् — ओंकारस्य रहस्यम्। यस्य वेद — तस्य सर्वम् वेद।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: The secret teaching of the Udgitha is the knowledge of Om as Brahman. One who knows this secret knows everything — for Om is the symbol of the all-knowing Brahman.

verses.sanskrit: आदित्यो ब्रह्मेत्यादेशः | तस्योपव्याख्यानम् ||३.१९.१||
verses.transliteration: ādityo brahmety ādeśaḥ | tasyopavyākhyānam ||3.19.1||
verses.english_translation: The Sun is Brahman — this is the teaching. Its explanation follows.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: आदित्यः — सूर्यः — ब्रह्म — इत्यादेशः — उपदेशः।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: The Sun is Brahman — this is the teaching of the Chandogya. The Sun is the most visible manifestation of Brahman in the cosmos. Its light, warmth, and life-giving power are expressions of Brahman's infinite power.

verses.sanskrit: एतदमृतमभयमेतद्ब्रह्म | एतद्ब्रह्मणो नाम सत्यमिति ||८.३.४||
verses.transliteration: etad amṛtam abhayam etad brahma | etad brahmaṇo nāma satyam iti ||8.3.4||

word_meanings.meaning: the name of Brahman is truth

verses.english_translation: This is immortal, this is fearless, this is Brahman. The name of this Brahman is truth.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: एतत् — ब्रह्म — अमृतम् — अभयम्। ब्रह्मणः नाम — सत्यम्।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: Brahman is immortal and fearless. Its name is truth (satya). Truth is the highest name of Brahman because Brahman is the ultimate reality — that which never changes, never perishes.

verses.sanskrit: तत् त्वम् असि श्वेतकेतो ||६.८.७||
verses.transliteration: tat tvam asi śvetaketo ||6.8.7||
verses.english_translation: That thou art, O Shvetaketu.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: तत् — जगत्कारणं ब्रह्म। त्वम् — प्रत्यगात्मा। असि — अभिन्नः।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: 'Tat' is Brahman, the cause of the universe. 'Tvam' is the individual Self. 'Asi' declares their identity. This mahavakya teaches the non-difference of the individual soul and Brahman.

verses.sanskrit: ओमित्येतदक्षरमुद्गीथमुपासीत | ओमिति ह्युद्गायति ||१.१.१||
verses.transliteration: om ity etad akṣaram udgītham upāsīta | om iti hy udgāyati ||1.1.1||
verses.english_translation: One should meditate on the syllable Om as the Udgitha. For one sings the Udgitha beginning with Om.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: उद्गीथः — सामगानस्य प्रधानभागः। ओंकारः तस्य वाचकः।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: The Udgitha is the principal part of Sama chanting. Om is its indicator. One should meditate on Om as the Udgitha, knowing it as Brahman.

verses.sanskrit: सर्वं खल्विदं ब्रह्म | तज्जलानिति शान्त उपासीत ||३.१४.१||
verses.transliteration: sarvaṃ khalv idaṃ brahma | tajjalāniti śānta upāsīta ||3.14.1||

word_meanings.meaning: from it born, into it dissolved, in it breathing

verses.english_translation: All this is indeed Brahman. From it all is born, into it all dissolves, in it all breathes. Meditate on it peacefully.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: सर्वम् — जगत् — ब्रह्मैव। तज्जलान् — तस्मात् जायते — तस्मिन् लीयते — तस्मिन् श्वसिति।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: All this universe is Brahman alone. 'Tajjalan' means: from it (taj) all is born (ja), into it all dissolves (la), in it all breathes (an). Meditate on this Brahman peacefully.

verses.sanskrit: मनोमयः प्राणशरीरो भारूपः सत्यसंकल्प आकाशात्मा सर्वकर्मा सर्वकामः ||३.१४.२||
verses.transliteration: manomayaḥ prāṇaśarīro bhārūpaḥ satyasaṃkalpa ākāśātmā sarvakarmā sarvakāmaḥ ||3.14.2||
verses.english_translation: Made of mind, whose body is prana, whose form is light, whose resolve is truth, whose Self is space, who performs all actions, who fulfills all desires.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: मनोमयः — मनःस्वरूपः। सत्यसंकल्पः — यस्य संकल्पः सत्यः। आकाशात्मा — आकाशस्वरूपः।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: This describes the inner Self (Brahman) as made of mind, with prana as its body, light as its form, truth as its resolve, and space as its Self. This is the meditation on the Self within the heart.

verses.sanskrit: एष म आत्मान्तर्हृदये | एतद्ब्रह्म | एतमितः प्रेत्याभिसंभवितास्मि ||३.१४.४||
verses.transliteration: eṣa ma ātmāntar hṛdaye | etad brahma | etam itaḥ pretyābhisaṃbhavitāsmi ||3.14.4||

word_meanings.meaning: this is my Self within the heart

verses.english_translation: This is my Self within the heart. This is Brahman. Into this I shall enter after departing from here.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: एषः — हृदयस्थः आत्मा — ब्रह्म। एतम् — इतः प्रेत्य — अभिसंभवितास्मि — ब्रह्मैव भविष्यामि।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: The Self within the heart is Brahman. After departing from this body, I shall become that Brahman. This is the declaration of the knower of Brahman — the certainty of liberation.

verses.sanskrit: आकाशो वै नाम नामरूपयोर्निर्वहिता | ते यदन्तरा तद्ब्रह्म ||८.१४.१||
verses.transliteration: ākāśo vai nāma nāmarūpayor nirvahitā | te yad antarā tad brahma ||8.14.1||

that which is between them is Brahman

verses.sanskrit: य आत्मापहतपाप्मा विजरो विमृत्युर्विशोको विजिघत्सोऽपिपासः सत्यकामः सत्यसंकल्पः ||८.७.१||
verses.transliteration: ya ātmāpahatapāpmā vijaro vimṛtyur viśoko vijighatsoapipāsaḥ satyakāmaḥ satyasaṃkalpaḥ ||8.7.1||

word_meanings.meaning: free from old age and death
whose desire and resolve are truth

verses.english_translation: The Self that is free from sin, free from old age, free from death, free from grief, free from hunger and thirst, whose desire is truth, whose resolve is truth.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: अपहतपाप्मा — पापरहितः। विजरः — जरारहितः। विमृत्युः — मृत्युरहितः। सत्यकामः — सत्यम् एव कामः यस्य।

verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: पुत्रैषणा — पुत्रस्य इच्छा। वित्तैषणा — धनस्य इच्छा। लोकैषणा — स्वर्गस्य इच्छा। एताः त्यक्त्वा — भिक्षाचर्यम् चरन्ति।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: Having known the Self, the wise renounce the three desires — for sons (continuation), wealth (security), and worlds (heaven). These three desires bind one to samsara. Renouncing them, one lives as a mendicant, seeking only liberation.

verses.sanskrit: यो वै ज्येष्ठं च श्रेष्ठं च वेद ज्येष्ठश्च श्रेष्ठश्च स्वानां भवति | प्राणो वाव ज्येष्ठश्च श्रेष्ठश्च ||५.१.१||
verses.transliteration: yo vai jyeṣṭhaṃ ca śreṣṭhaṃ ca veda jyeṣṭhaś ca śreṣṭhaś ca svānāṃ bhavati | prāṇo vāva jyeṣṭhaś ca śreṣṭhaś ca ||5.1.1||

word_meanings.meaning: prana is the eldest and best

verses.english_translation: One who knows the eldest and best becomes the eldest and best among his own. Prana is indeed the eldest and best.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: ज्येष्ठः — सर्वेषाम् प्रथमः। श्रेष्ठः — सर्वेषाम् उत्तमः। प्राणः — ज्येष्ठः श्रेष्ठः — सर्वेषाम् इन्द्रियाणाम्।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: Prana is the eldest and best among all the senses and faculties. One who knows prana as Brahman becomes the eldest and best — the most honored — among his own people.

verses.sanskrit: उद्गीथस्योपनिषदं वेद | यस्य वेद तस्य वेद ||१.१०.९||
verses.transliteration: udgīthasya upaniṣadaṃ veda | yasya veda tasya veda ||1.10.9||

whatever belongs to that one, he knows

verses.english_translation: One who knows the secret teaching of the Udgitha — whatever belongs to that one, he knows.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: उद्गीथस्य उपनिषत् — ओंकारस्य रहस्यम्। यस्य वेद — तस्य सर्वम् वेद।

verses.transliteration: ādityo brahmety ādeśaḥ | tasyopavyākhyānam ||3.19.1||
verses.english_translation: The Sun is Brahman — this is the teaching. Its explanation follows.
verses.shankara_bhashya_sanskrit: आदित्यः — सूर्यः — ब्रह्म — इत्यादेशः — उपदेशः।
verses.shankara_bhashya_english: Shankara: The Sun is Brahman — this is the teaching of the Chandogya. The Sun is the most visible manifestation of Brahman in the cosmos. Its light, warmth, and life-giving power are expressions of Brahman's infinite power.

verses.sanskrit: एतदमृतमभयमेतद्ब्रह्म | एतद्ब्रह्मणो नाम सत्यमिति ||८.३.४||
